column1: # fia_cornered_clusters.tsv — (ECOREGION_PREFIX <TAB> SIGNATURE) pairs whose divergences have been
# BOTH-SIDES-TRACED and cornered as an accepted taxonomy class (not a fixable bug). The coverage sweep's
# dig-worthy extractor (filter_digworthy.jl) DROPS a candidate row when its stand's ECOREGION starts with
# a listed prefix AND its signature matches — so the sweep advances to NEW strata instead of re-pausing on
# an already-cornered cluster every ~200 stands. ESCALATION GUARD (never dropped, always re-surfaced):
#   • signature == UNCLASSIFIED  (needs a manual trace regardless of cluster)
#   • a STRUCTURE/density blow-up: worst_col ∈ {TPA,BA,SDI,CCF,TopHt,QMD} AND max_rel_pct >= 15
#     (the cornered classes keep density within ~1 unit; a real density bug would exceed this and re-surface)
# Add a row here only after a dig session documents the corner in docs/FIA_FVS_COMPAT_AUDIT.md.

column4: Central Appalachian (221*) dense mixed-hardwood: cyc-0 bit-exact, sub-print Float32 DBH-growth accumulation compounds; merch/board-volume tail. Forest-type reclassification proven bit-exact. Verified 221H (dig#1/#2, many stands) + 221Ja (dig#2d, 163384065010854: bit-exact 1980, 0.8% seed 1985 identical TPA). Real volume-eq bugs still surface (TCuFt escalation guard); UNCLASSIFIED + structure blow-ups (TPA/BA/SDI/CCF/QMD >=15%) still escalate.

Central Appalachian (221*) dense mature/young stands near SDImax: self-thinning count-straddle (a few TPA) + sawtimber merch-threshold crossings on ~zero volume bases. Density (BA/SDI/CCF) preserved within ~1 unit. Verified 221H + 221Ja same taxonomy. Escalation guard still surfaces genuine structure/volume-eq bugs anywhere in 221*.
